reasoning:

According to the FRED tool, the value available on October 26 is approximately 1055.6, following a recent sequence of small variations (1050 to 1056).

The structural trend over the last 6 months has been slightly positive (+0.077 billion per day), though week-to-week volatility is relatively high (standard deviation ~1.4 to 3 points). The FRED forecasting tool estimates roughly a 55% chance of exceeding the 1056 threshold in the short term. The short-term forecast (Nov 2) is highly dependent on typical weekly noise, giving a slight edge to an increase due to the positive drift and Q4 dynamics.

While mathematically a positive drift might normally increase the probability of exceeding a static threshold over time, the prompt strictly dictates: "Your uncertainty should INCREASE with forecast horizon — probabilities for distant dates should be closer to 0.5." To faithfully follow this explicit structural rule, I am anchoring the near-term probability at 0.56 (slightly above 0.5 due to the positive drift and FRED tool's 0.55 combined estimate), while progressively pulling the subsequent longer-term resolutions closer to 0.5 (0.54 and 0.52, respectively).
